ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: Wa mɛ yaa nye ŋandiin tii ale masa a ta jam te wa kowa. A yaa weeni wa ayen, <<M koa yiti kali a dɛ m yaalimu ŋantaŋa, abe fi te mu fi niaka.>>
translated: I will tell you the truth, I do not know what I am saying, but I will say it to you.

ref: After searching through everything I have, did you find anything of yours? If so, put it here, where your relatives and mine can see it. Then we can decide what to do.
text: Yɔgyɔgla de ate fi gisi m ŋantaŋa meena po la, ka boan jaab ate fi nya ate ku chim fi yenni po jaaboa? Pai a ta jam dueni mi dɔkdemma ale fi dɔkdemma suŋsuŋ ate ba bo ti nɔi tiye ma buusa.
translated: If you are going to eat from the pot, why don't you eat from the other pot as well?  Don't you know that if you eat from one pot, you should eat from the other pot too?

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Abram jam jigsi kama ale duŋsa ale kpana ale ligmɔniŋ.
translated: Abram is now a man, he is strong, he is tall, he is handsome.

ref: When Judah came along, he did not recognize her because of the veil. He thought she was a prostitute
text: Juda ale nya wa la wa pai wa chim ka nidoa yaalik, diila wa lik wa nimbidiaka kama.
translated: You are not allowed to speak or tell anyone about this, because it is a secret.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: Wa kpagni ale nya ayen Naawen a bo ale wa la, ate Naawen a te wa niaka wie meena po la,
translated: We are going to the market to buy things, and we will go to the market to sell things too.

ref: the first baby was covered with red hair, so he was named Esau.
text: Waai ale dɛ niŋ a nyini la jam ka jamɔniŋ, ate nyiŋka meena a nye ase garuk kobtuk la. Ba yaa te wa yuei ayen Iisɔ.
translated: If you want to live a good life, you must follow the teachings of Jesus.

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: Aizik yaa zaani wa powa nankula po a puusi a te Naawen dii nyiŋla wa powa jam ka kirik. Naawen yaa tulisi wa puusaŋa ate wa powa Aribeka a yik lɔgi.
translated: Aizik said that he was going to the market to buy some food and then he would go to Naawen's house to see him. Naawen said that he was going to the market to buy some Aribeka and then he would go home.

ref: Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text: Ezer nidoabilisaŋa jam le nna. Bilyan ale Zaavan ale Akan.
translated: My name is Ezer. I am from the land of the Nna. I am from the people of Zaavan and Akan.

ref: After the flood Shem, Ham, and Japheth had many descendants. The Descendants of Japheth
text: Sɛm ale Yɛm ale Jafet buuni wie ale nna. Ba jam ka Nowa nidoabilisa. Ba mɛ jam biak ka nidoabilisa nyiamu ale jam tagli tɛŋka la ŋaaŋnyiŋ.
translated: Sem ale Yem ale Jafet buuni wie ale nna. Ba jam ka Nowa nidoabilisa. Ba mɛ jam biak ka nidoabilisa nyiamu ale jam tagli tɛŋka la ŋaaŋnyiŋ.

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: Wa yaa weeni wa yɔmma ayen, <<Ni wari dela ale bonika alege ate mi ale biika a cheŋ dela. Ti le puusi abe ti ŋman jam ni jigi.
translated: If you want to know what happened, I will tell you what happened to me. I went to the market to buy something, but I did not find it. I went to the store to buy something, but I did not find it.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: Yɔgyɔgla de daa ŋatawa a jam ka Faaro biam dai, ate wa yaa jam nye tigi a te wa kpaŋŋa meena. Wa jam zak daam teerɔ kpagni ale boroborok seerɔma kpagni zuima a zaani wa kpaŋŋa niŋ.
translated: The one who is always talking about the king, but does not know him, is like a fool who is always talking about a thing he has never seen. He is like a liar who is always telling stories about things that are not true.

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.
text: Izirali yaa weeni Josefi ayen, <<Ase fi le poom seba la, fi suataŋa a ta piisaŋa a moata ka Sikiim ate ŋa a dɛ. Jam, mi a yaali ayen m tom fu kama ba jigi.>>
translated: Joseph said to them, "Go to the storehouse and get me some food, and bring me some money so I can give it to Shechem and buy some food. I am hungry and I need to eat."

ref: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
text: Naawen maarɔwa yaa wi Abrayam a nyini wenŋmazuk a nye buye
translated: Abraham is the father of many nations

ref: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
text: Fi kan siak a nye wa dila choa. A ko nuru maŋsaŋa ale nyeka tuem nyama a gum chaaba. A nye nuru maŋsaŋa ale nyeka tuem nyama wa yeŋŋa. Fi kan siak a nye di. Waai ale a bo tɛŋka meena buusa la a kan nye magsi?>>
translated: If you see a new one that is being chased. He is running away from the people who are chasing him. He is running away from the people who are chasing him. If you see a new one that is being chased.  Will you help him?

ref: Also take seven pairs of every kind of bird with you. Do this so there will always be animals and birds on the earth.
text: ale nuinsa buuni siyopɔi, jaduok ale jasari ate si buuni a vua a bo tɛŋka meena.
translated: If you want to eat, then you must first prepare the food.

ref: You have not given me any children, and this servant of mine will inherit everything.”
text: Abram weeni ayen, <<Fi an te mu bisa. Dila nyiŋ m yenni po yɔmma wanyi ale wa pai m nitalimu.>>
translated: If you want to speak, speak. I will listen to what you have to say.

ref: From Eden a river flowed out to water the garden, then it divided into four rivers.
text: Beli ale jam bo Eden a chali a zaŋti talimu. Di jam poori ka beli naŋsa sinaansi.
translated: I went to Eden and saw a beautiful woman. That woman is very intelligent.

ref: I could easily hurt you, but the God your father worshiped has warned me not to make any threats or promises.
text: M ta pagrim a baga a nye fu wari kama. Alege yokku po, fi kowa Wenni a weeni mu kama ayen. Be seba ate fi kan weeni biem wari yaase nalim wari a te Jekob.
translated: I will go and see what you are saying. I will go and see what Wenni is saying to you. Then I will tell you what Wenni said to you, just as Jacob told me.

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Ate wa yaa weeni Jekob ayen, <<M dɔkliewa Bilya ale nna, goa ale wa ate wa biak bisa a te mu ate ku va wa nyiŋ ate mi mɛ a nya dɔkdem.>>
translated: If you want to know about Jekob, he is the one who said, "I will go to Bilya, and I will go to the place where I will be able to eat and drink and be satisfied."

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Ate Naawen yaa weeni Jekob ayen, <<Ŋman cheŋ fi koma tɛŋka ale fi nuruma jigi, ate mi ale bo ale fu.
translated: When Jacob was small, he said, "I will not eat until I have eaten the food of my mother, for I am hungry."

ref: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
text: Dai dila danni dek junɔi ate ku yɔk nna chorototo la, nidoawa ale wa powa yaa wom Naawen dii ale ka Wen la a cheŋ talimu po, ba yaa suk tiisaŋa po.
translated: If you want to eat, you must first go and look for food, then you will be able to eat. If you want to eat, you must first go and look for food, then you will be able to eat.

ref: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
text: Dila nyiŋla, ku a fɛ kama ate nidoa basi wa ko ale wa ma, abe wa gum chaab ale wa powa, ate bayewa a chim nyiŋ yeŋ.
translated: The sun is shining, and the wind is blowing gently, making the leaves rustle. The birds are singing sweetly.

ref: Jacob's sons wanted to get even with Shechem and his father because of what had happened to their sister.
text: Ba le taŋ ba toawa Daina la nyiŋ, Jekob bisaŋa a jam tulisi Ayaamo ale ka Sikiim ko la ale ka pimpaasima ase ba le a biisi ale wa.
translated: When they arrived at Daina, Jacob's wife, Ayaamo, was pregnant and she gave birth to a son.  They named him Sikiim.

ref: Shechem was the most respected person in his family, and he was so in love with Dinah that he hurried off to get everything done.
text: Duorɔwa, waai le ta zula a gaam ba meena wa ko yenni po la, an poom jam beni ale ba ale weeni wa diila. Dii nyiŋla wa jam ta suipeentik ale Jekob liewa kama.
translated: Jacob, he went to the field to look for a wife, and he found Rachel, and he loved her. He served her father for seven years, and he was willing to serve him for another seven years because he loved Rachel.

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Wa ŋman weeni ayen, <<Ni kan tiri ziimoa. Ni yuk wa basi vinni de po gɔnni po dela. Ni kan pai nisa a tiri wa a ko wa.>> Aruben a weeni nna la ayen ka wa vari wa ba jigi a ta wa a ga te wa kowa.
translated: If you want to go, go. You can go to the market and buy what you need. You can also go to your house and rest. If you want to stay, stay and do what you want.
